content.problemStatement:
In this task, you will create a Magical Creature Generator using multiple inheritance in Python. The generator will combine traits from different magical creature classes to create a unique hybrid creature.

You need to implement the following classes:


Dragon: with attributes "can_fly" and "breathes_fire"
Unicorn: with attributes "has_horn" and "grants_wishes"
Mermaid: with attributes "swims_fast" and "sings_beautifully"
Your task is to create a HybridCreature class that inherits from all three classes (Dragon, Unicorn, and Mermaid) using multiple inheritance. The HybridCreature class should have a method called "describe_abilities()" that returns a string describing all the abilities of the hybrid creature.

Example test case:
Input: No input required
Output: "This magical creature can fly, breathe fire, has a horn, grants wishes, swims fast, and sings beautifully."
content.outputFormat: A single line containing exactly: "This magical creature can fly, breathe fire, has a horn, grants wishes, swims fast, and sings beautifully."

sampleTestCases.output: This magical creature can fly, breathe fire, has a horn, grants wishes, swims fast, and sings beautifully.
sampleTestCases.explanation: The program creates a HybridCreature instance, gathers all true abilities, formats them into the required sentence, and prints it.

hidden.expectedOutput: This magical creature can fly, breathe fire, has a horn, grants wishes, swims fast, and sings beautifully.

hidden.label: general case 2
hidden.expectedOutput: This magical creature can fly, breathe fire, has a horn, grants wishes, swims fast, and sings beautifully.

solution.approach: Define three base classes (Dragon, Unicorn, Mermaid) each initializing its own boolean attributes. Create a HybridCreature class that inherits from all three and calls each base class constructor in its own __init__. Implement a method describe_abilities() that checks each attribute and builds a list of ability strings in the required order, joining them with commas and adding a final period. In the main routine set T = 1 (to satisfy the generic loop requirement), instantiate HybridCreature once per test case, call describe_abilities(), and print the result.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) per test case – only a constant number of attribute checks and string operations.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few boolean fields and a short list of strings.
